# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: linkerd-server
---
apiVersion: extensions/v1beta1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  labels:
    app: linkerd-server # this is the service name for this microservice. Change accordingly based on microservice name
    version: v1
  name: linkerd-server # this is the service name for this microservice. Change accordingly based on microservice name
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 2 # number of replicas to be created for this microservice in K8s cluster. Can change, but don't change the label.
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 1
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: linkerd-server # this is the service name for this microservice. Change accordingly based on microservice name
        version: v1
apiVersion: extensions/v1beta1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  namespace: default
  name: linkerd-server # this is the service name for this microservice. Change accordingly based on microservice name
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: linkerd-server # this is the service name for this microservice. Change accordingly based on microservice name
      version: v1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: linkerd-server # this is the service name for this microservice. Change accordingly based on microservice name
        version: v1
    spec:
      containers:
      - imagePullPolicy: Always # we are using latest tag of linkerd-proxy. Change it to image tag (e.g 1.5.0) or specify a proper linkerd image.
        image: docker.io/buoyantio/linkerd-proxy-api-server:1.4.2
        name: linkerd # this is the name of linkerd sidecar that is used for authentication by this microservice. Can be changed as necessary.
        ports:
          - containerPort: 80
            protocol: TCP
      serviceAccountName: linkerd-server
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: serverauthentications.linkerd.io
spec:
  group: linkerd.io # Don't change the group name. It is a part of K8s CRD standards.
  names:
    kind: ServerAuthentication # Don't change this, it's the kind of CRDs. Kind are used to retrieve objects from api server.
    listKind: ServerAuthenticationList # Don't change this, it's the kind that shows all CRDs. 
    plural: serverauthentications # This name can be changed to a unique value
    singular: serverauthentication
  scope: Namespaced # This is to ensure that our CRD is namespace specific. We do not want the same CRD applied across multiple namespaces in K8s cluster.
  subresources: {} # Don't change this. It's how we are using server side validation here. Not using server-side validation will result in error during deployment.
  validation:
    openAPIV3Schema:
      properties:
        spec:
          properties:
            serviceAccountRef:
              description: Name of the k8s ServiceAccount that this proxy should use for authentication
              type: string # It's a name, not an object so it's type is string
            servers:
              description: The list of servers to be authenticated. 
              items:
                description: A server in an auth-policy. All authentication requests will match at least one server spec and not all servers.
                properties:
                  ca:
                    description: Optional PEM encoded CA that will be used for TLS verification on servers
                    type: string
                  name: # Don't change this either, it's name of k8s Service that is being targeted in auth-policy
                    description: Name of a Kubernetes service whose endpoints should be authenticated
                    type: string
                  port:  # Do not change the port number here. Otherwise it will result in error during deployment due to integer conversion problem on linkerd side. 
                    type: int
                required: [name]
              type: array
            serverServiceAccountsRef: 
              description: Optional name of the k8s ServiceAccount that this proxy should use for authentication. This can be used instead of serviceAccountRef if you wish to share an authentication secret among multiple services. 
              type: string
          type: object
      required: [serviceAccountRef, servers]
  versions:
    - name: v1 # Don't change the version here because we want to implement the CRD for server side authorization. 
      served: true
      storage: false
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: serviceaccounts.linkerd.io
spec:
  group: linkerd.io # Don't change this, it is a part of K8s CRD standards. 
  names:
    kind: ServiceAccount
    listKind: ServiceAccountList 
    plural: serviceaccounts 
    singular: serviceaccount # This can be changed to a unique value as long as we follow the standards.
  scope: Namespaced # This is to ensure that our CRD is namespace specific. We do not want the same CRD applied across multiple namespaces in K8s cluster. 
  validation:
    openAPIV3Schema:
      properties:
        spec:
          type: object
            spec:
              serviceAccountsRef:
                description: Optional name of a Kubernetes service account whose tokens will be included as JWT access tokens for proxy requests to services. If not present, a randomly generated token will be provided.
                type: string
              serviceIdentityRef: 
                description: The name of the k8s ServiceIdentity that identifies this ServiceAccount in the proxy’s identity cache (e.g. "sa-1"). Required if spec.serviceAccounts is omitted. 
                type: string 
              servicePortIndex: # This property will be used to map port number from K8s service port to linkerd proxy port. If both are same, it can be skipped.
                type: int
      required: [serviceIdentityRef]
    served: true 
    storage: false
  versions:
    - name: v1 
      served: true
      storage: false
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: serviceidentities.linkerd.io
spec:
  group: linkerd.io # Don't change this, it is a part of K8s CRD standards. 
  names:
    kind: ServiceIdentity 
    listKind: ServiceIdentityList 
    plural: serviceidentities 
    singular: serviceidentity # This can be changed to a unique value as long as we follow the standards.
  scope: Namespaced # This is to ensure that our CRD is namespace specific. We do not want the same CRD applied across multiple namespaces in K8s cluster. 
  validation:
    openAPIV3Schema:
      properties:
        spec:
          serviceAccountsRef: 
            description: Optional name of a Kubernetes service account whose tokens will be included as JWT access tokens for proxy requests to services. If not present, a randomly generated token will be provided.
            type: string # It's name that we are using to reference the k8s serviceAccount resource. 
          servicePortIndex:  # This property will be used to map port number from K8s service port to linkerd proxy port. If both are same, it can be skipped. 
            type: int
        served: true 
        storage: false
      required: [serviceAccountsRef]
    served: true 
    storage: false
  versions:
    - name: v1 
      served: true
      storage: false
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: auth-policies.linkerd.io
spec:
  group: linkerd.io # Don't change this, it is a part of K8s CRD standards. 
  names:
    kind: AuthPolicy 
    listKind: AuthPolicyList 
    plural: auth-policies 
    singular: authpolicy  # This can be changed to a unique value as long as we follow the standards.
  scope: Namespaced # This is to ensure that our CRD is namespace specific. We do not want the same CRD applied across multiple namespaces in K8s cluster. 
  validation:
    openAPIV3Schema:
      properties:
        spec:
          rules:
            description: The set of rules for how requests should be authenticated at a given server.
              items:
                description: Rule defines a rule to which an incoming request is matched by the incoming request’s identity. Only one of all the matchers in the rule will match and, if not present, every request matches. A match must include both identity and ports. 
                properties:
                  clientIdentity:
                    description: Optional JWT claims that are required for requests that match this rule to be authenticated. These are claims on the requesting side that can be matched by service identity of a proxy. Only one of all the matchers in the rule will match and, if not present, every request matches
                    items:
                      type: string # It's a list so we use array for it here as well.
                    type: array
                  ports: 
                    description: The set of ports on which authentication requests must match to be authenticated at this server.
                    items: 
                      properties:
                        portIndex:
                          type: int 
                        portName: 
                          type: string
                            portIndex # This property will be used to map port number from K8s service port to linkerd proxy port. If both are same, it can be skipped. 
                          type: int
                      required:[portIndex]
                    type: array
                  selector:
                    description: The selector matches a set of k8s Services that are being targeted by this AuthPolicy’s ServerAuthentication. Only one of all the matchers in the rule will match and, if not present, every Service matches 
                    properties: 
                      serviceAccountsRef: 
                        description: Optional name of a Kubernetes service account whose tokens will be included as JWT access tokens for proxy requests to services. If not present, a randomly generated token will be provided.
                        type: string
                      servicePortIndex: # This property will be used to map port number from K8s service port to linkerd proxy port. If both are same, it can be skipped. 
                        type: int
                    required:[serviceAccountsRef]
                  serverNameRegex: 
                    description: Optional regular expression pattern that the serverName of the Server must match to be authenticated at this server.
                    type: string
                  serversRef: # The name of K8s Service that is being targeted in AuthPolicy resource
                    description: Name of a Kubernetes service whose endpoints should be authenticated
                    type: string
                required:[clientIdentity, ports]
              type: array
          version:
            description: Version defines the configuration for this auth policy. These rules will only be applied to requests on the configured servers at version and higher versions. This field can only be set when spec.rules is empty
            type: integer
      required:[rules]
    served: true 
    storage: false
  versions:
    - name: v1 
      served: true
      storage: false
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: server-apis
spec: # this is the kind of resource that is being exposed in k8s cluster for microservices to communicate with each other. 
  group: linkerd.io  # Don't change the group here. It's part of standard for K8s CRDs and it must be the same as that used above under metadata
  names:
    kind: ServerAPI
    plural: server-apis # This name can be changed to a unique value 
    singular: server-api 
  scope: Namespaced  # We want our custom resource for microservices deployed in each namespace. It should not be available at global level. 
  version: v1alpha1
  versions: []
status: {}
